Package cc.quaternion.potshaper

Class Pot3DFormat.Rotation

java.lang.Object
cc.quaternion.potshaper.Pot3DFormat.Rotation
Enclosing class:
Pot3DFormat
public static class Pot3DFormat.Rotation extends Object
Represents a 3D rotation configuration for a pot.

This class defines rotation angles around the X, Y, and Z axes. It provides a fluent API to set these angles individually or together. The Z-axis rotation is automatically normalized to a value between 0 and 360 degrees.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    double
    getRotationX()
    Returns the current rotation angle around the X-axis.

    Returns the current rotation angle around the Y-axis.

    Returns the current rotation angle around the Z-axis.

    Sets both the X and Y rotation angles simultaneously, typically used for perspective adjustments.

    Sets the rotation angle around the X-axis.

  • Method Details

    • x

      public Pot3DFormat.Rotation x(double degrees)
      Sets the rotation angle around the X-axis.

      This method updates the X-axis rotation and returns the same Rotation instance to allow for method chaining.

      Parameters:
      degrees - the rotation angle in degrees
      Returns:
      this Rotation instance for chaining

      Usage Example

      
 Rotation rot = Pot3DFormat.rotation().x(45.0).y(30.0);

    • y

      public Pot3DFormat.Rotation y(double degrees)
      Sets the rotation angle around the Y-axis.

      This method updates the Y-axis rotation and returns the same Rotation instance to allow for method chaining.

      Parameters:
      degrees - the rotation angle in degrees
      Returns:
      this Rotation instance for chaining

      Usage Example

      
 Rotation rot = Pot3DFormat.rotation().y(20.0).z(90.0);

    • z

      public Pot3DFormat.Rotation z(double degrees)
      Sets the rotation angle around the Z-axis.

      This method updates the Z-axis rotation and returns the same Rotation instance to allow for method chaining. The input angle is taken modulo 360 degrees, ensuring the result is within a standard rotational range.

      Parameters:
      degrees - the rotation angle in degrees
      Returns:
      this Rotation instance for chaining

      Usage Example

      
 Rotation rot = Pot3DFormat.rotation().z(450.0); // Result is 90.0 degrees

    • perspective

      public Pot3DFormat.Rotation perspective(double x, double y)
      Sets both the X and Y rotation angles simultaneously, typically used for perspective adjustments.

      This is a convenience method that sets the X and Y rotations in one call, returning the same instance for chaining. It is equivalent to calling x(x).y(y).

      Parameters:
      x - the rotation angle around the X-axis in degrees
      y - the rotation angle around the Y-axis in degrees
      Returns:
      this Rotation instance for chaining

      Usage Example

      
 Rotation rot = Pot3DFormat.rotation().perspective(30.0, 15.0);

    • getRotationX

      public double getRotationX()
      Returns the current rotation angle around the X-axis.
      Returns:
      the X-axis rotation in degrees

    • getRotationY

      public double getRotationY()
      Returns the current rotation angle around the Y-axis.
      Returns:
      the Y-axis rotation in degrees

    • getRotationZ

      public double getRotationZ()
      Returns the current rotation angle around the Z-axis.
      Returns:
      the Z-axis rotation in degrees, normalized to [0, 360)
